channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump calls off ceasefire and criticizes Iran's leaders weeks after praising them
item.description: President Trump praised Iranian leaders just weeks ago. Now, he's called them "cuckoo" and says the ceasefire is over.
item.content:encoded: President Trump praised Iranian leaders just weeks ago. Now, he's called them "cuckoo" and says the ceasefire is over.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Sen. Jeanne Shaheen talks about Trump at NATO and the renewed strikes against Iran
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with New Hampshire Sen. Jeanne Shaheen about President Trump at the NATO summit and more strikes in the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with New Hampshire Sen. Jeanne Shaheen about President Trump at the NATO summit and more strikes in the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Federal judge orders release of $5.8M payment to E. Jean Carroll
item.description: A federal judge in Manhattan ordered the payment of $5.8 million to writer E. Jean Carroll from a sexual abuse and defamation case against President Trump. The payment had been held up by appeals.
item.content:encoded: A federal judge in Manhattan ordered the payment of $5.8 million to writer E. Jean Carroll from a sexual abuse and defamation case against President Trump. The payment had been held up by appeals.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Repairs continue at New York building after structural failure prompted evacuation
item.description: Structural problems at a building in Midtown Manhattan continue to snarl traffic as repairs and inspections proceed. The area was evacuated this week when the building was deemed at risk of collapse.
item.content:encoded: Structural problems at a building in Midtown Manhattan continue to snarl traffic as repairs and inspections proceed. The area was evacuated this week when the building was deemed at risk of collapse.
item.dc:creator: David Brand

item.title: A Florida airport is officially renamed for Trump. What does he stand to gain?
item.description: Trump is the first president to have an airport named after him while in office. The Trump Organization says he won't get royalties from the renaming, but legal experts see potential loopholes.
item.content:encoded:
Trump is the first president to have an airport named after him while in office. The Trump Organization says he won't get royalties from the renaming, but legal experts see potential loopholes.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump criticizes Iran's leaders and says ceasefire is over, Middle East countries prepare for the potential of more war as U.S. and Iran renew strikes, Graham Platner drops his bid for Senate.
item.content:encoded: Trump criticizes Iran's leaders and says ceasefire is over, Middle East countries prepare for the potential of more war as U.S. and Iran renew strikes, Graham Platner drops his bid for Senate.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy talks about his new memoir
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Dave Portnoy, the founder of Barstool Sports, about his new memoir "Cancel Me If You Can."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Dave Portnoy, the founder of Barstool Sports, about his new memoir "Cancel Me If You Can."
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Oil and gasoline prices begin to climb after Trump says ceasefire with Iran is over
item.description: Oil and gasoline prices are climbing again after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran. That could put more upward pressure on inflation.
item.content:encoded: Oil and gasoline prices are climbing again after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran. That could put more upward pressure on inflation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Community calls for answers after fatal shooting of unarmed Houston man by ICE agent
item.description: Several immigrant rights organizations are demanding an independent investigation into a fatal ICE shooting in Houston.
item.content:encoded: Several immigrant rights organizations are demanding an independent investigation into a fatal ICE shooting in Houston.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Seward

item.title: Department of Justice charges three major egg suppliers with price fixing
item.description: Consumers were alarmed by high egg prices last year. The DOJ's case against three major egg producers confirms that a price fixing scheme may have been to blame.
item.content:encoded: Consumers were alarmed by high egg prices last year. The DOJ's case against three major egg producers confirms that a price fixing scheme may have been to blame.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
